author: chelseafc
text:
FT: Chelsea 1-1 Newcastle 🔵

Kubo with a 96th-minute equaliser! Point taken, hearts not okay. #CHENEW

author: skysports
text:
FT: Chelsea 1-1 Newcastle.

Takefusa Kubo scores deep into stoppage time to rescue a point for Xabi Alonso's side, who drop to 5th in the Premier League table after a mixed month domestically. Focus now shifts to Wednesday's Champions League second leg. #CHENEW

author: kubo
text: So so happy for that goal today, right at the end 🙏 Never stop believing. Big week ahead now 💙

author: optajoe
text: Takefusa Kubo has now scored a 90th-minute-or-later goal in back-to-back Chelsea appearances. A player finding his moment at exactly the right time of the season. ⏱️

author: terry
text: 96th minute. NINETY-SIXTH. My heart cannot keep doing this. Relieved, genuinely relieved, but conceding a stoppage-time near-miss at home to Newcastle after Arsenal and Villa is not what title-chasing sides do. In my day 1-0 up meant game over.

author: sue
text: I have aged several years in second-half stoppage time three times this month. Kubo, I owe you my cardiovascular health. 5th place though. FIFTH. Send tea, send biscuits, send Xanax.

author: terry
text: Right. Two league defeats, a 96th-minute let-off, and we're 5th. Be honest with yourselves for once:

author: danny
text: 96th MINUTE KUBO. MY HEART. Look, it's not the position we want, 5th stings, but this kid just will not stop scoring when it matters. Massive week now — Galatasaray, then Everton, then WEMBLEY. Let's go. 💙

author: marcus
text: Officially: two different Chelseas exist right now. Cup Chelsea (Birmingham, Galatasaray) is fearless, ruthless, ice-cold in big moments. League Chelsea (Arsenal, Villa, and nearly today) is shakier, more mistake-prone. Whichever one turns up Wednesday decides our season. 🧵

author: gaz
text: 5th place. FIFTH. Chelsea fans were measuring up the trophy cabinet in February 😂 Enjoy the Europa League race lads.

author: chelseafc
text:
FULL-TIME: Galatasaray 1-3 Chelsea 🔵⚽

What a night in Istanbul. Came from behind to take control of the tie. Second leg at the Bridge next week. #UCL

author: skysports
text:
FT: Galatasaray 1-3 Chelsea (Champions League Round of 16, first leg).

Chelsea go behind in a cauldron atmosphere and respond in ruthless fashion — João Neves at the heart of everything again. A huge result to take into the second leg at Stamford Bridge. #GALCHE

author: neves
text: Difficult atmosphere, difficult start, but we showed real character 💪 Big result to take home. Now let's finish the job at the Bridge 🔵

author: optajoe
text: João Neves has been the standout performer in both of Chelsea's last two away performances. 5 duels won, 2 key passes tonight in Istanbul. Undroppable right now. ⭐

author: danny
text: WENT TO ISTANBUL, WENT BEHIND, AND STILL WON 3-1. NEVES AGAIN. I'm not saying we're back back, but WE MIGHT BE BACK BACK. 😭💙 #GALCHE

author: sue
text: Cautiously, carefully, with one eye on the exit in case this jinxes it: that might be our best away performance of the season. Second leg at home. I'm allowing myself a SLIVER of hope.

author: marcus
text: 1-0 down at Galatasaray, in that atmosphere, and we controlled the rest of the game. 2.1 xG for us after going behind. This team has a spine now — it doesn't panic. That's new, and it's the biggest takeaway from tonight.

author: gaz
text: Fine, credit where due, that's a proper away result in Europe. Doesn't fix your league form though 👀

author: ornstein
text: Sources at Chelsea describe tonight as the proof-of-concept moment for Xabi Alonso's project on the European stage — going behind away from home in a hostile atmosphere and finishing the stronger side. Second leg preview to follow. @TheAthleticFC

author: chelseafc
text:
FULL-TIME: Chelsea 3-0 Birmingham City 🔵🏆

Into the FA Cup semi-finals! Goals from Kubo (2) and João Pedro. Massive response from the boys. #FACup

author: skysports
text:
FT: Chelsea 3-0 Birmingham City (FA Cup QF).

Exactly the response Xabi Alonso needed — Takefusa Kubo scores twice, João Neves is imperious in midfield, and 20-year-old Máximo Perrone impresses on his full debut start. Chelsea into the FA Cup semi-finals. #FACup

author: kubo
text: Two goals today 🙏 Big win for the team when we needed it most. On to the semis! 💙

author: neves
text: Great response from the group today 💪 Proud of this team. Semi-final next, vamos!

author: perrone
text: My first start for this club and I'll never forget this feeling. Thank you Xabi for the trust, thank you to my teammates. Onto the semi-final 💙🔵

author: optajoe
text: Takefusa Kubo's first multi-goal game for Chelsea. João Neves: 91 touches, 6 tackles, a match-high 7 progressive carries. Máximo Perrone (full debut start): 94% pass accuracy, 3 interceptions. A statement performance from the fringe players. 📈

author: danny
text: THAT'S the response. Kubo with TWO, Neves controlling everything, Perrone looking like he's been starting all season. Two bad results does not erase six months of work. Semi-final, let's go. 💙

author: terry
text: Good response, credit due. Kubo and Perrone were excellent — genuinely excellent, not just 'good for a fringe player'. Still want to see it against a top-half side away from home before I get carried away again, but a proper reaction. About time.

author: fabrizio
text: Understand Enzo Fernández was withdrawn at half-time as a precaution after a knock — nothing serious, more a rest given the schedule ahead. Expected to be available for Galatasaray. #CFC

author: chloe
text: Kubo brace, Neves masterclass, Perrone looking born for this. Gaz being suspiciously quiet today 👀

author: chelseafc
text:
FT: Aston Villa 2-1 Chelsea 🔵

Enzo Fernández with our goal. Two defeats on the spin now. Regroup for Birmingham. #AVLCHE

author: skysports
text:
FT: Aston Villa 2-1 Chelsea.

A second successive Premier League defeat for Xabi Alonso's side, despite a battling display from Robert Sánchez and a goal from Enzo Fernández. Villa move above Chelsea in the table. #AVLCHE

author: sanchez
text: Hard one to take. Gave everything out there tonight. We pick ourselves up, FA Cup on Sunday. 💙🧤

author: optajoe
text: Robert Sánchez made 7 saves tonight, his joint-highest tally of the season, in a losing effort. Enzo Fernández created 4 chances, more than any other player on the pitch. Not enough. 📊

author: terry
text: TWO in a row now. Villa away, fair enough, tough place to go — but this is exactly what I warned about in February. You cannot run a squad on vibes forever. Sánchez was immense, Enzo was immense, the rest of it needs a serious look before Sunday.

author: sue
text: Two losses in four days. I have officially entered the 'checking the table every hour' phase of the season. Send Kubo off the bench sooner too, while we're at it.

author: danny
text: Not gonna lie, this one stings more than Arsenal. Sánchez kept us in it, Enzo was everywhere, and we still find a way to lose it. Birmingham on Sunday, then Galatasaray. We NEED a response. I still believe. I think. 💙

author: chloe
text: Genuinely the first time all season I've felt actual nerves instead of my usual delusional confidence. Sánchez was excellent tonight though, credit where due. Big week coming, let's see what we're made of.

author: chelseafc
text:
FT: Arsenal 3-0 Chelsea 🔵

A tough afternoon at the Emirates. Back to work. #ARSCHE

author: skysports
text:
FT: Arsenal 3-0 Chelsea.

A harsh reality check for Xabi Alonso's side at the Emirates — comfortably second best from the first whistle. Gonçalo Inácio was one of the few positives on a difficult day. #ARSCHE

author: optajoe
text: Chelsea have now lost to Arsenal in both Premier League meetings this season, conceding 3+ goals in each. Gonçalo Inácio: 6 clearances, 4 interceptions, 91% pass accuracy — no other Chelsea defender got close. 🛡️

author: terry
text: 3-0. At Arsenal. AGAIN. I said in February the table doesn't lie forever and here we are. Same old story away at the big boys. Inácio's the only one who turns up with his boots on. In my day we at least made them work for it.

author: gaz
text: Chelsea fans quiet today 😴 Same old Chelsea when it actually matters. Enjoy the Carabao Cup lads, we'll take the league, thanks. 🔴 #ARSCHE

author: sue
text: 3-0. At the Emirates. I want it on record that I have been saying 'don't get carried away' since November and NOBODY LISTENS TO ME. Anyway. Onwards, apparently.

author: danny
text: Rough one. Really rough one. Not gonna pretend that wasn't bad. But — Galatasaray Wednesday, Birmingham Sunday, still fighting on three fronts. We regroup. 💙 (I'm allowed one quiet day, don't take this from me too)

author: marcus
text: Sorry, but I have to be honest with you all: that wasn't unlucky, that was deserved. 1.4 xG against 2.6 for them. Second balls, half-yard reactions, Arsenal won all of it. No stats trick out of this one. We were beaten by the better team today.

author: skysports
text:
One month from today: Chelsea vs Manchester City, Carabao Cup final, Wembley 🏆

Who's lifting it? #CarabaoCup

author: danny
text: 54%??? FIVE STRAIGHT WINS. SIX PAST WOLVES. WENT TO THE EMIRATES AND KNOCKED THE LEAGUE LEADERS OUT TO GET THERE. The disrespect is actually fuel at this point. See you at Wembley 😤💙

quote.author: skysports
quote.text: One month from today: Chelsea vs Manchester City, Carabao Cup final, Wembley 🏆 Who's lifting it?

author: ornstein
text: 🚨 Special report: Inside Xabi Alonso's Chelsea rebuild — 12 signings, 8 major departures (including Cole Palmer and Reece James), one non-negotiable idea. Sources at Cobham describe the clearest tactical identity the club has had in a decade. Full piece @TheAthleticFC

author: terry
text: Right. Five wins on the bounce, a cup final booked. Time to re-run the most controversial poll in this account's history. Be honest:

author: sue
text:
March: Arsenal away. Villa away. Cup quarter-final. Newcastle. Everton away. And then a final. Against City. At Wembley.

I have never felt so alive and so nauseous at the same time.

author: marcus
text:
Chelsea in February: P5 W5. 12 scored, 3 conceded.

A cup semi won at the Emirates, a six-goal away day, two grown-up 1-0s. The underlying numbers finally match the eye test — this team peaks at the exact right moment of the season. 🧵👇

author: optajoe
text:
14 & 14 - Enzo Fernández and João Pedro have both reached 14 goals in all competitions for Chelsea this season. Supply lines: João Neves and Anthony Gordon, 8 assists each.

Symmetry. 💙

author: enzo
text: Otra victoria más 💙 Gracias Bridge, siempre con nosotros. Ahora el Emirates. VAMOS!

author: gordon
text: Five in a row. Bridge was rocking again 💙 Big March ahead. On to the next one.

author: chelseafc
text:
FT: Chelsea 1-0 Burnley 🔵

Five wins on the spin. Clean sheet. Job done.

Next up: the Emirates. 💪 #CHEBUR

author: terry
text:
1-0 at home to Burnley and the timeline is planning the trophy parade. It's BURNLEY. Win at the Emirates next Sunday and THEN I'll smile.

(Good three points, mind. Clean sheet. Cubarsí immense again. But standards.)

author: chloe
text: Genuinely obsessed with the fact we can bring Kubo, Nusa AND Yılmaz off the bench now. Squad depth used to mean "pray nobody gets injured". Now it's a flex. Xabi built an army.

author: fabrizio
text: 🚨🔵 Understand Chelsea have opened internal talks over a new long-term contract for Moisés Caicedo — club consider him untouchable and the heart of Xabi Alonso's project. No release clause planned. More soon. #CFC

author: sue
text:
Saw Cole Palmer trending tonight and had a tiny breakdown about it, as is tradition.

Then I remembered we've won every game this month and Enzo has FOURTEEN goals. Healing isn't linear but it is happening.

author: optajoe
text:
🧱 No Chelsea player has more ball recoveries, progressive carries out of defence or completed passes into the final third this season than Moisés Caicedo.

The anchor of everything. #CFC

author: chloe
text: Mid-February check-in, cup final loading. Player of the season so far — and be serious, not just your favourite 💙

author: chelseafc
text:
FT: Grimsby Town 0-1 Chelsea 🔵

Into the FA Cup quarter-finals — Birmingham City at the Bridge next month. 🏆 #FACup

author: terry
text:
Away at Grimsby. February. Freezing. Pitch like a farmer's field. 1-0, everyone home safe, into the quarters.

THAT is a proper cup performance. In my day we'd have drawn it and lost the replay under the lights. Progress.

author: skysports
text:
FT: Chelsea 2-1 Leeds.

Three wins in a week for Xabi Alonso's side, who stay firmly in the top four and keep the pressure on the leaders. #CHELEE

author: marcus
text:
Wolves 1-6 Chelsea: 4.9 xG. A record in my spreadsheet, which goes back to 2016.

26 box touches for the front line. Gordon and Neves involved in everything. I ran the numbers twice because I did not believe them the first time.

author: joaopedro
text: What a night 🔥 SIX goals away from home?? This team is something special. Deus é fiel 🙏💙

author: gaz
text: Wolves are in a relegation scrap and Chelsea fans are acting like they just won the Champions League 😂 wake me up when you do it against an actual top side

thread.author: chloe
thread.text: The Emirates. February 3rd. Cup semi-final. 2-1. You were there Gaz, we all saw your story x

author: danny
text: SIX. AWAY FROM HOME. IT COULD HAVE BEEN TEN. THIS IS THE BEST CHELSEA SIDE I'VE SEEN SINCE I WAS A KID AND I'M NOT EVEN EXAGGERATING FOR ENGAGEMENT 😭💙 #WOLCHE

author: skysports
text:
FT: Wolves 1-6 Chelsea.

SIX for the Blues at Molineux — the biggest away win of the Xabi Alonso era. Ruthless, relentless, terrifying. #WOLCHE

author: ornstein
text: Understand there is genuine belief inside Chelsea this squad can compete on every front this season. Hierarchy see the Carabao final as the moment the rebuild announces itself + last-16 Champions League place secured directly via top-6 league-phase finish exceeded internal expectations @TheAthleticFC

author: danny
text: WEMBLEYYYYY 😭😭💙 WE BEAT THEM IN THEIR OWN HOUSE. THE REBUILD HAS A FINAL. Booking March 22 off work the second HR opens tomorrow.

author: terry
text:
Beating Arsenal at their place in a cup semi is proper Chelsea. PROPER Chelsea.

Now listen carefully because I've seen this film: it means NOTHING if we don't finish the job against City. Ask anyone with an old season ticket.

author: sue
text:
I cried a little at full-time. Do not quote-tweet this.

We beat Arsenal, at theirs, in a semi-final, with half a team that was signed six months ago. Whatever happens at Wembley — this project is real.

author: gaz
text: Carabao Cup is a Mickey Mouse trophy anyway. We're clear at the top of the ACTUAL competition. Enjoy your little day out at Wembley x

thread.author: danny
thread.text: Mickey Mouse trophy and yet you played your strongest XI and your whole end went silent at the second goal. Interesting! 💙

author: chelseafc
text:
FULL-TIME: Arsenal 1-2 Chelsea 🔵

WE ARE GOING TO WEMBLEY!!! 🏆💙 #CarabaoCup

author: skysports
text:
FT: Arsenal 1-2 Chelsea (Carabao Cup semi-final).

Chelsea knock the league leaders out at the Emirates and will face Manchester City in the final at Wembley on March 22. A statement from Xabi Alonso's side. #ARSCHE
